rationale: Bitcoin Cash follows Bitcoin's identical halving schedule; the most recent halving occurred in April 2024, reducing the block reward from 6.25 BCH to 3.125 BCH per block (~144 blocks/day). With ~450 BCH minted daily against a circulating supply of approximately 19.87 million BCH (per CoinGecko), the annualized new issuance is ~164,250 BCH, yielding an inflation rate of roughly 1.72%. The next halving is expected around April 2028, which will cut the rate to ~0.86%, so the trend over the next 24 months remains flat-to-declining as supply grows and the halving approaches.
